Sexually Transmitted Infections Treatment Guidelines, 2021

Human Papillomavirus (HPV) Infection 
Print 
Minus 
Related Pages 
Pages in this Section 
Anogenital Warts 
HPV-Associated Cancers and Precancers 
Approximately 150 types of HPV have been identified, at least 40 of which infect the genital area ( 1194 ). The majority of HPV infections are self-limited and are asymptomatic or unrecognized. Sexually active persons are usually exposed to HPV during their lifetime ( 838 , 1195 , 1196 ). Oncogenic, high-risk HPV infection (e.g., HPV types 16 and 18) causes the majority of cervical, penile, vulvar, vaginal, anal, and oropharyngeal cancers and precancers ( 1197 ), whereas other HPV infect
